body_txt: A character, or more often a whole group of people, has something elementary and basic explained to them, but for some reason, they just aren't getting it. They aren't being deliberately obtuse and stupid . They simply do not understand the rather obvious implications of what has been said to them. To the character trying to do the explaining, and to the audience, the train of thought is painfully easy to make work logically, but the target simply isn't getting it. Usually, this is because the concept is so foreign to them that they cannot grasp it. This is not a case of What Is This Thing You Call "Love"? , where an alien species has no concept of the idea, but rather a case of a greedy character not understanding the concept of charity, or someone with a Complexity Addiction failing to grasp a Mundane Solution . Sometimes this is because of Selective Obliviousness or Obfuscating Stupidity , but only rarely does the audience know this at the time. Compare Sustained Misunderstanding , Cassandra Truth , and Evil Cannot Comprehend Good . Contrast with Too Dumb to Fool .
